content.problemStatement:
Chefland consists of nnn cities numbered 111, 222, ‚Ä¶n\ldots n‚Ä¶n. The cities numbered 111, 222, ‚Ä¶k\ldots k‚Ä¶k are special cities. For each unordered pair i,ji, ji,j, there exists a bidirectional road, destroying which has a cost of wi,jw_{i, j}wi,j‚Äã. Find the minimum cost of destroying some subset of roads so that no two special cities have a path between them after the removal of these roads.


Input Format
There are multiple independent testcases.



First line will contain TTT, number of testcases.
The first line of each testcase contains nnn and kkk, the number of cities and the number of special cities respectively.
The jthj^{\text{th}}jth integer in ithi^{\text{th}}ith of the next nnn lines is wijw_{ij}wij‚Äã.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the minimum cost of destroying some subset of roads so that no two special cities have a path between them after the removal of these roads.
constraints: The sum of nnn over all testcases doesn't exceed 202020.
